solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def canBeEqual(self, target: List[int], arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        return sorted(arr) == sorted(target)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The problem allows reversing any subarray any number of times, which effectively allows rearranging elements into any permutation. Thus, checking if both arrays can be sorted to the same sequence confirms they can be made equal. Sorting both arrays and comparing them ensures correctness with optimal efficiency.
